title: 날씨가 좋군요

goal.ko: 날씨를 비교하고, 들은 정보를 전달할 수 있어요.

annotation.note:
보다 (to see) → 뵙다 (to see, honorific)
오랜만에 = after a long time
어른들끼리 쓰는 정중한 표현이에요!

script.speaker: mirae_mom
script.translation: Thanks to you, I've been doing well.

annotation.note:
덕분에 = thanks to (you / someone)
L1 ep11에서 배운 표현!
'덕분에 잘 지내요' — 정중한 인사의 정석이에요.

script.speaker: liam_mom
script.translation: This year isn't as hot compared to last year.

annotation.note:
작년에 비해서 = compared to last year
N에 비해서 = compared to N
기준점을 제시할 때 써요!

script.speaker: mirae_mom
script.translation: I know, right. It's much cooler than last year, isn't it?

annotation.note:
작년보다 훨씬 선선해요 = much cooler than last year
N보다 = more than N
훨씬 = much more (강조)
-에 비해서보다 더 직접적인 비교예요!

script.speaker: liam_mom
script.text: 뉴스에서 봤는데 북쪽에서 찬바람이 내려오고 있대요.
script.translation: I saw on the news that cold wind is coming down from the north.

annotation.note:
내려오고 있대요 = (they say) it's coming down
-대요 = -다고 해요 (줄임)
뉴스·누군가에게 들은 정보를 전달할 때 써요!

script.speaker: mirae_mom
script.translation: I heard cold air is blocking the monsoon front — is that right?

annotation.note:
막고 있다면서요? = I heard it's blocking — is that right?
-다면서요? = 들은 내용을 확인할 때
= '그렇다고 하던데, 맞아요?' 뉘앙스예요!

script.speaker: liam_mom
script.translation: Anyway, I hope next year is as cool as this year.

annotation.note:
올해처럼 시원해요 = as cool as this year
N처럼 = like N / as N
유사함을 표현할 때 써요!

script.speaker: mirae_mom
script.text: 이제 곧 장마와 함께 뜨거운 공기가 밀려올 거래요.
script.translation: They say hot air will soon surge in along with the monsoon.

annotation.note:
밀려올 거래요 = (they say) it will surge in
-거래요 = -(으)ㄹ 거라고 해요 (줄임)
미래에 대한 전달 표현이에요!

script.speaker: liam_mom
script.text: 작년같이 더울까요? 작년에는 한 달 내내 폭염이었잖아요.
script.translation: Will it be as hot as last year? Last year was a heat wave all month long, you know.

annotation.note:
작년같이 = like last year (-처럼과 같은 뜻!)
폭염이었잖아요 = it was a heat wave, you know
-잖아요 = '우리 둘 다 알고 있잖아요' 뉘앙스!

script.speaker: mirae_mom
script.translation: They say it'll be even hotter than last year.

annotation.note:
더울 거라고 해요 = they say it will be hot
-다고 해요 = 간접화법 (indirect speech)
-대요 / -거래요 의 풀어쓴 형태예요!

script.speaker: liam_mom
script.translation: Anyway, global warming is a real problem.

annotation.note:
지구 온난화 때문에 = because of global warming
N 때문에 = because of N
원인을 나타내요!

grammar.title: -에 비해서 / -보다
grammar.explanation_en: Two main ways to compare in Korean. -에 비해서 sets a reference point ('compared to X'); -보다 directly states more/less ('more than X').

scene_example.english: This year isn't as hot compared to last year.

rule_boxes.content:
작년에 비해서 선선해요 = cool compared to last year
올해에 비해서 더워요 = hot compared to this year
기준점을 제시할 때 — more formal
rule_boxes.label: -에 비해서 (compared to)

rule_boxes.content:
작년보다 훨씬 선선해요 = much cooler than last year
작년보다 더 더울 거예요 = will be hotter than last year
더 직접적인 비교 — very common in speech
rule_boxes.label: -보다 (more than)

rule_boxes.content:
훨씬 = much more (강한 강조)
더 = more (일반 강조)
작년보다 훨씬 더워요 vs 작년보다 조금 더워요
rule_boxes.label: 강조 부사

items.en: It's not as hot compared to last year.
My Korean has improved a lot compared to before.
It's much cooler than last year, right?
They say it'll be even hotter than last year.

grammar.title: -다면서요?
grammar.title_en: Confirming hearsay: 'I heard that... is it true?'
grammar.explanation_en: Use -다면서요? when you've heard something and want to confirm it with the listener. It carries the nuance of 'I heard X — is that right?'

scene_example.english: I heard cold air is blocking the monsoon front — is that right?

rule_boxes.content:
동사 현재: -ㄴ/는다면서요? (가다→간다면서요?)
동사 과거: -았/었다면서요? (갔다면서요?)
형용사: -다면서요? (덥다면서요?)
N이다: N(이)라면서요? (학생이라면서요?)
rule_boxes.label: 형태 만들기

rule_boxes.content:
'들었는데 맞아요?' = I heard it — is it true?
확인·재확인할 때 사용
= -다고 하던데, 맞아요? 와 같은 뜻
rule_boxes.label: 뉘앙스

rule_boxes.content:
반말: -다면서? (장마전선을 막고 있다면서?)
존댓말: -다면서요? (막고 있다면서요?)
이 에피소드는 어른들끼리라서 -요 필수!
rule_boxes.label: 반말 vs 존댓말

examples.en: I heard it's going to rain tomorrow — is that right?
I heard Bomi lives in Seoul — is that right?
I heard cold air is blocking the monsoon front?

grammar.title: -대요 / -거래요 / -다고 해요
grammar.explanation_en: All three mean 'they say / I heard that.' -대요 and -거래요 are shortened spoken forms of -다고 해요. Use them to pass on information you heard from someone else.

scene_example.english: They say cold wind is coming down from the north.

rule_boxes.content:
내려오고 있대요 = they say it's coming down
덥대요 = they say it's hot
갔대요 = they say she went
= -다고 해요 줄임
rule_boxes.label: -대요 (현재/과거 전달)

rule_boxes.content:
밀려올 거래요 = they say it will surge in
비가 올 거래요 = they say it will rain
= -(으)ㄹ 거라고 해요 줄임
rule_boxes.label: -거래요 (미래 전달)

rule_boxes.content:
더울 거라고 해요 = they say it will be hot
뉴스에서 그렇다고 해요 = the news says so
말할 때는 -대요/-거래요가 더 자연스러워요!
rule_boxes.label: -다고 해요 (풀어쓴 형태)

items.en: I saw on the news that cold wind is coming down.
They say it'll rain a lot tomorrow.

items.ko: 장마와 함께 뜨거운 공기가 밀려올 거래요.
items.en: They say hot air will surge in with the monsoon.
They say this summer will be especially hot.

grammar.title: -처럼 / -같이
grammar.explanation_en: Both -처럼 and -같이 mean 'like' or 'as.' They are interchangeable in most contexts and express similarity or comparison.

scene_example.english: I hope next year is as cool as this year.

rule_boxes.content:
올해처럼 시원해요 = as cool as this year
작년같이 더울까요? = will it be as hot as last year?
둘 다 'like / as' 의미로 교환 가능!
rule_boxes.label: -처럼 / -같이 (interchangeable)

rule_boxes.content:
-보다 = more than (정도 비교)
-에 비해서 = compared to (기준 비교)
-처럼/-같이 = like, as (유사 비교)
rule_boxes.label: 비교 표현 총정리

examples.en: I hope it's as cool as this year.
Will it be as hot as last year?
I want to cook as well as my mom.

items.note: ㅝ 모음 — 영어 'wuh' 비슷. '훠얼씬'처럼 늘어지지 않도록!
긴 복합명사. 장마 / 전선 두 단어로 끊어 읽기

banmal_jondaemal.intro_ko: 이 에피소드는 처음부터 끝까지 어른들끼리 존댓말을 사용해요. 반말 버전으로 비교해 볼게요.
banmal_jondaemal.intro_en: This episode uses formal speech throughout. Compare with casual speech below.

messages.translation: Isn't the weather really cool today?
I know~ It's much better compared to last year!

messages.text: 뉴스에서 봤는데 이번 주 내내 이럴 거래.
messages.translation: I saw on the news that it'll be like this all week.

real_life.title: 날씨 뉴스 읽기
real_life.passage: 올해 여름은 작년에 비해서 선선한 편입니다. 기상청에 따르면 북쪽에서 찬공기가 내려오고 있어서 장마가 늦어지고 있다고 합니다. 하지만 전문가들은 장마가 시작되면 작년보다 더 더울 수 있다고 경고하고 있습니다.
real_life.passage_en: This summer is on the cooler side compared to last year. According to the meteorological agency, cold air is coming down from the north, causing the monsoon to be delayed. However, experts warn that once the monsoon begins, it could be hotter than last year.

self_check: -에 비해서/-보다 를 써서 날씨를 비교할 수 있어요.
-다면서요? 로 들은 정보를 확인할 수 있어요.
-처럼/-같이 로 유사 비교를 할 수 있어요.

quiz.question: Which expression means 'compared to last year'?
quiz.question_ko: 작년을 기준으로 비교할 때 맞는 표현은?

quiz.question: What does 라온 엄마 say about this year's weather?
options: It's not as hot compared to last year
It's the same as last year

quiz.question: 보미 엄마 heard cold air is blocking the monsoon front. Which expression does she use to CONFIRM this?
quiz.question_ko: 들은 정보를 확인할 때 어떤 표현을 써요?

quiz.question: What does 라온 엄마 hear on the news?
quiz.audio_text: 뉴스에서 봤는데 북쪽에서 찬바람이 내려오고 있대요.
options: Hot wind is coming from the south
Cold wind is coming down from the north

quiz.question: What do they say about next year's heat?
options: It'll be cooler than last year
It'll be the same as last year
It'll be even hotter than last year

usage.heading: 날씨 표현 총정리
usage.content: Korean weather vocab is rich — pair these with the grammar patterns in this episode.

usage.heading: 뉴스·일기예보 듣기 표현
usage.content: When watching Korean weather forecasts, you'll often hear these patterns.

examples.en: They say temperatures are lower compared to last year.
They say clear days like today will continue.
